title: Leucophae infernalis
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called "Leucophae infernalis". However, I think you might be referring to "Leucophane infernalis" (more likely) or possibly "Leucophyllum frutescens". 

If you're referring to "Leucophyllum frutescens", I can provide you with some information about it:

Leucophyllum frutescens, also known as Texas sage or Texas barometer bush, is a flowering shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and improving respiratory health. The bioactive constituents of Leucophyllum frutescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as topical creams and salves for skin health.
